tool.name: dashing2 sketch
tool.description: summarize sequence or feature collections and optionally compare them
tool.command:
#import re ## dashing2 reads a list of input paths from a file-of-files (-F). We ## symlink every collection element under a sanitized copy of its ## element_identifier so the names that appear in the distance matrix ## are the dataset names, and so spaces (which dashing2 treats as a ## record separator inside an FFILE line) cannot corrupt the list. mkdir -p '@FFILE_DIR@' && #for $element in $input_type.input_collection: #set $name = re.sub('[^A-Za-z0-9._-]', '_', str($element.element_identifier)) ln -s '$element' '@FFILE_DIR@/${name}' && echo '@FFILE_DIR@/${name}' >> 'ffile.txt' && #end for #set $use_query = $compare.enabled == 'yes' and $compare.query.use_query == 'yes' #if $use_query: #for $qelement in $compare.query.query_collection: #set $qname = re.sub('[^A-Za-z0-9._-]', '_', str($qelement.element_identifier)) ln -s '$qelement' '@FFILE_DIR@/${qname}' && echo '@FFILE_DIR@/${qname}' >> 'qfile.txt' && #end for #end if
dashing2 sketch -p \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-8} -S${sketch_params.sketch_size} #if $input_type.itype == 'bed': --bed $input_type.normalize_intervals #elif $input_type.itype == 'bigwig': --bigwig $input_type.normalize_intervals #elif $input_type.itype == 'leafcutter': --leafcutter #else: $input_type.alphabet #end if $sketch_mode #if str($sketch_params.kmer_length) != '': -k${sketch_params.kmer_length} #end if #if str($sketch_params.window_size) != '': --window-size ${sketch_params.window_size} #end if #if str($sketch_params.spacing) != '': --spacing '${sketch_params.spacing}' #end if $sketch_params.no_canon $sketch_params.long_kmers #if str($sketch_params.seed) != '': --seed ${sketch_params.seed} #end if $parse_by_seq ## The stacked-sketch output (-o) only works for FASTA/FASTQ in this ## dashing2 build; for BED/BigWig/LeafCutter it aborts, so those ## input types produce the comparison matrix only. #if $input_type.itype == 'fasta': -o '$sketch_output' #end if #if $compare.enabled == 'yes': $compare.measure #if not $use_query: $compare.result_shape #end if #if $compare.sparse.mode == 'topk': --topk ${compare.sparse.topk} #elif $compare.sparse.mode == 'threshold': --similarity-threshold ${compare.sparse.threshold} #end if $compare.binary_output #if $use_query: -Q 'qfile.txt' #end if --cmpout '$distance_output' #elif $input_type.itype != 'fasta': --cmpout '$distance_output' #end if -F 'ffile.txt'

param.name: itype
param.label: Input type
param.help: The kind of data being sketched. FASTA/FASTQ sequences are the common case. BED, BigWig and LeafCutter inputs are sketched as weighted interval/coverage sets.

param.name: input_collection
param.label: Sequence collection
param.help: A collection of FASTA or FASTQ datasets (optionally gzip-compressed). Each dataset becomes one sketch, unless 'Parse each sequence separately' is enabled.

param.name: input_collection
param.label: BED collection
param.help: A collection of BED datasets. Each dataset is sketched as a set of reference base/contig intervals.

param.label: Normalize intervals
param.help: Give every interval equal weight instead of weighting by its length.

param.name: input_collection
param.label: BigWig collection
param.help: A collection of BigWig datasets, sketched as weighted coverage vectors.

param.label: Normalize intervals
param.help: Give every interval equal weight instead of weighting by its coverage.

param.name: input_collection
param.label: LeafCutter collection
param.help: A collection of LeafCutter splicing-output datasets, sketched as weighted junction sets.

param.label: Parse each sequence separately
param.help: Treat every sequence within every input file as a separate entity (rather than one sketch per file). Required for protein homology search, edit-distance grouping and per-sequence clustering.

param.name: enabled
param.label: Compute distances after sketching
param.help: When enabled, dashing2 additionally compares the sketches and writes a distance/similarity matrix (the --cmpout step). When disabled, only the stacked sketch file is produced. This toggle applies to FASTA/FASTQ input; BED, BigWig and LeafCutter inputs always produce the distance matrix.

param.name: use_query
param.label: Rectangular query-vs-reference comparison
param.help: Compare every input above (the reference set) against a separate query collection, producing a rectangular |reference|×|query| matrix instead of an all-pairs matrix.

param.name: query_collection
param.label: Query collection
param.help: Datasets forming the query set. Each is compared against every dataset in the reference collection.

tool.help:
dashing2 sketch summarizes a collection of sequences (or BED / BigWig / LeafCutter feature sets) into compact **sketches** and, optionally, compares those sketches to produce a distance or similarity matrix.
A sketch is a small probabilistic representation of the set (or multiset) of k-mers in a dataset. Comparing sketches estimates set similarities such as the Jaccard index orders of magnitude faster than comparing the full sequences, and scales to millions of inputs through locality-sensitive hashing (LSH).
-----
Input *****
A **dataset collection**. Each element becomes one sketch, unless *Parse each sequence separately* is enabled, in which case each individual sequence becomes its own entity. The element names are carried through to the row/column labels of the distance matrix.
Four input types are supported:
============ ============== =========================================== **Type** **Flag** **Notes** ------------ -------------- ------------------------------------------- FASTA/FASTQ (default) Nucleotide or protein; gzip accepted BED --bed Interval sets; optional normalization BigWig --bigwig Weighted coverage vectors LeafCutter --leafcutter Weighted splicing-junction sets ============ ============== ===========================================
-----
Sketching mode **************
The sketch data structure determines how k-mer multiplicities are handled.
============================ ============= =========== ==================================== **Mode** **Flag** **Default** **Treats inputs as** ---------------------------- ------------- ----------- ------------------------------------ One-permutation SetSketch (none) Yes Sets (fastest) Full SetSketch --full No Sets (better for large sketches) Exact k-mer set --set No Exact sets (sorted hash set) Exact k-mer count dictionary --countdict No Exact multisets ============================ ============= =========== ====================================
The weighted-sequence sketching modes (BagMinHash / ProbMinHash) are not offered here because they crash in the packaged dashing2 release. For weighted data use the BED / BigWig / LeafCutter input types above, or the *dashing2 wsketch* tool.
-----
Options *******
=========================== ================ ============ =========================== **Option** **Flag** **Default** **Description** --------------------------- ---------------- ------------ --------------------------- K-mer length -k alphabet max Length of hashed k-mers Sketch size -S 1024 Registers per sketch Window size --window-size k Minimizer winnowing window Spaced-seed pattern --spacing unspaced Ignored/used positions Disable canonicalization --no-canon False Strand-specific DNA 128-bit k-mers --long-kmers False Up to 64 bp DNA k-mers Hash seed --seed default Hash function seed Parse each sequence --parse-by-seq False One entity per sequence =========================== ================ ============ ===========================
-----
Comparison **********
When *Compute distances after sketching* is enabled, the sketches are compared with ``--cmpout``. The **measure** can be similarity (default), Mash/Poisson distance, containment, symmetric containment, intersection size or union size. The **output shape** is a symmetric upper-triangular PHYLIP matrix by default, a square ``--asymmetric-all-pairs`` matrix, or a rectangular matrix when a query collection is supplied (``-Q``). Sparse output limits the matrix to each item's top-k nearest neighbours (``--topk``) or to pairs above a similarity threshold (``--similarity-threshold``), emitting a compressed-sparse-row result. ``--binary-output`` writes float32 binary instead of text.
-----
Use cases *********
========================================== ================================================= **Goal** **How to select it** ------------------------------------------ ------------------------------------------------- All-pairs distance matrix Enable comparison (default shape/measure) Top-k nearest-neighbour graph Comparison + sparse Top-k Similarity-thresholded graph Comparison + sparse threshold Protein homology search Protein alphabet + parse each sequence Weighted feature comparison BED / BigWig / LeafCutter input, or wsketch Exact k-mer KNN with LSH prefilter --set / --countdict + sparse Top-k ========================================== =================================================
-----
Output ******
For FASTA/FASTQ input, a **sketches** dataset (stacked binary sketches, usable as input to *dashing2 dist*) is produced, plus a **distances** dataset when comparison is enabled. There is no native Galaxy datatype for dashing2 sketches, so they are stored as ``binary``.
BED, BigWig and LeafCutter inputs do not support the reusable sketch output in this dashing2 release, so for those input types only the **distances** dataset is produced (the comparison always runs).
